content.problemStatement:
For an integer xxx, define its digit space DxD_xDx‚Äč to be the set of all positive integers whose decimal representation is a rearrangement of the decimal representation of xxx, when written without leading zeros.

For example, if x=2024x = 2024x=2024, then Dx={4022,4202,4220,2042,2024,2402,2420,2204,2240}D_x = \{4022, 4202, 4220, 2042, 2024, 2402, 2420, 2204, 2240\}Dx‚Äč={4022,4202,4220,2042,2024,2402,2420,2204,2240}.

For two integers xxx and yyy, define f(x,y)f(x, y)f(x,y) to be the maximum **prime** number that divides both xxx and yyy.

If no prime divides both xxx and yyy, we say f(x,y)=1f(x, y) = 1f(x,y)=1 instead.

For instance, f(35,105)=7f(35, 105) = 7f(35,105)=7 and f(2,3)=1f(2, 3) = 1f(2,3)=1.

You are given two integers XXX and YYY.

Compute the maximum value of f(a,b)f(a, b)f(a,b) across all (a,b)(a, b)(a,b) such that a‚ąąDXa\in D_Xa‚ąąDX‚Äč and b‚ąąDYb\in D_Yb‚ąąDY‚Äč.

That is, compute the maximum possible common prime factor of two numbers, such that one is a rearrangement of XXX and the other is a rearrangement of YYY.


Input Format

The first line of input will contain a single integer TTT, denoting the number of test cases.
Each test case consists of single line of input, containing two space-separated integers XXX and YYY.


Output Format
For each test case, output on a new line the maximum possible value of f(a,b)f(a, b)f(a,b) where a‚ąąDXa\in D_Xa‚ąąDX‚Äč and b‚ąąDYb\in D_Yb‚ąąDY‚Äč.
